questions.question:
Mala the Elephant keeps supply sacks with different weights.
They are all mixed up.

Why does Biru the Monkey suggest sorting them first?
options: So searching and picking becomes easier

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion compares neighboring sacks and swaps them if they are out of order.

Which sorting method is this?

Biru the Monkey tells Kaya to
pick the lightest sack first and place it at the front.

Which sorting method matches this idea?

Kaya the Lion watches Neel the Eagle insert feathers one by one into the correct position.

Which sorting method does this describe?

Kaya the Lion feels the sorting is slow.
Biru the Monkey suggests breaking the list into smaller parts and combining them later.

Which sort is this?

Biru the Monkey warns Kaya that merge sort needs another idea to work properly.

Which idea is required?

Kaya the Lion asks which sorting method is best for large data sets.

Which option is usually preferred?

Kaya the Lion writes bubble sort logic.
One line is missing.

Which option completes the logic?
questions.code:
```
FOR i from 0 to n-1:
    FOR j from 0 to n-i-2:
        IF array[j] > array[j+1]:
            _________
```

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion writes selection sort logic.
One line is missing.

Which option completes the logic?
questions.code:
```
minIndex = i
FOR j from i+1 to n-1:
    IF array[j] < array[minIndex]:
        _________
```

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion writes merge logic.
One line is missing.

Which option correctly completes it?
questions.code:
```
WHILE left < leftSize AND right < rightSize:
    IF leftArray[left] <= rightArray[right]:
        result[k] = leftArray[left]
        left = left + 1
    ELSE:
        result[k] = rightArray[right]
        _________
    k = k + 1
```
